name: Playtest 16: Fey Grove
description:
TESTS: All new lair action effect types: condition-only (Grasping Roots applies restrained via STR save), damage+condition combo (Freezing Wind deals cold + prone via CON save), healing (Nature's Mending heals 2d6 HP to enemy creatures), temp HP (Fey Ward grants 8 temp HP to enemies), summoning (Call of the Wild summons a wolf via monsters/wolf.json), AI scoring prioritizes different effect types correctly, summoned creature appears in initiative and on grid.

The party ventures into an ancient fey grove corrupted by a mage and her wolf guardians. The grove itself is alive with magic: roots grasp at intruders, freezing winds howl, nature mends the guardians' wounds, fey magic shields allies, and the grove calls wolves to defend it. AI-controlled (use_ai_for_enemies=true) to test AI scoring of all new lair effect types.

lair_actions.name: Grasping Roots
lair_actions.description: Twisted roots burst from the earth, entangling creatures in their grip.

lair_actions.name: Freezing Wind
lair_actions.description: An unnatural gale of freezing wind sweeps through the grove.

lair_actions.name: Nature's Mending
lair_actions.description: The grove channels restorative energy into its guardians.

lair_actions.name: Fey Ward
lair_actions.description: Shimmering fey magic wraps around the grove's defenders.

lair_actions.name: Call of the Wild
lair_actions.description: The grove summons a wolf from the forest to defend it.
